title: EMEA Firms Lack AI Visibility Despite Sovereignty Push, IBM Reports
optimized_headline: EMEA Firms Struggle with AI Transparency Amid Sovereignty Efforts, IBM Reveals
raw_excerpt: While most executives view AI sovereignty as a strategic imperative, many lack understanding of the infrastructure underpinning their systems.
raw_body: While most executives view AI sovereignty as a strategic imperative, many lack understanding of the infrastructure underpinning their systems.
speedrun: A new IBM report reveals that many executives in EMEA firms see AI sovereignty as essential, yet they struggle to grasp the infrastructure behind their AI systems. This disconnect suggests a gap in knowledge that could hinder effective implementation. With AI sovereignty becoming increasingly important, understanding these foundational elements is crucial for businesses to navigate the evolving landscape. The report highlights the urgency for firms to bridge this knowledge gap to leverage AI effectively.
why_it_matters: Firms risk falling behind if they cannot align their AI strategies with the necessary infrastructure, impacting their competitive edge.
This trend indicates a broader challenge in the market, as companies may struggle to adopt AI technologies effectively without a solid understanding of their systems.

lenses.eli12: Many business leaders believe having control over AI technology is important, but they often don't know how their systems actually work. It’s like wanting to drive a car without understanding how the engine functions. For everyday people, this means companies might not use AI to its full potential, which could affect services and products they rely on.
lenses.pm: For product managers and founders, this report highlights a critical user need: understanding the infrastructure behind AI systems. If teams lack this knowledge, it could lead to inefficient use of resources and missed opportunities. A practical implication is that investing in training or partnerships could help bridge this gap, enhancing product development and strategy.
lenses.engineer: From a technical perspective, the report underscores a significant issue: many executives lack insights into the infrastructure necessary for AI sovereignty. This could lead to poorly informed decisions regarding AI deployment and governance. Without a solid grasp of underlying systems, firms might struggle to optimize their AI capabilities, potentially affecting performance benchmarks and competitive positioning.
